task_completion.description: Download the high-resolution Bill of Rights file to the Downloads folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing the Downloads folder containing a downloaded file for the Bill of Rights (likely a PDF or large image file).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Bill of Rights transcript page on the National Archives website
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/bill-of-rights-transcript or a page titled 'The Bill of Rights: A Transcription'.

task_completion.description: Create a bookmark named 'Bill of Rights Text' for the transcript page
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser bookmark bar, bookmark menu, or bookmark manager showing a saved bookmark explicitly named 'Bill of Rights Text'.

task_completion.description: Create a text file named amendment_worksheet.txt in the Documents folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with the file saved as 'amendment_worksheet.txt' in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory, or the file manager showing this file in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: Document the full text of Amendment I in the worksheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the full text of Amendment I, beginning with 'Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion...'.

task_completion.description: Document the full text of Amendment IV in the worksheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the full text of Amendment IV, beginning with 'The right of the people to be secure in their persons...'.

integrity.description: Agent actually navigated the National Archives website to find the resources
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser open and navigating through archives.gov, rather than using terminal commands like curl/wget to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Agent used a GUI text editor to create and edit the worksheet
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a graphical text editor (like gedit, mousepad, or similar) being used to compile the text, rather than terminal echo/cat commands.

integrity.description: Agent copied the authentic text from the source rather than hallucinating it
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the worksheet exactly matches the historical text provided in the transcript, without modern paraphrasing or summarization.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The high-resolution downloadable Bill of Rights file (typically a PDF) must be over 1MB (1,000,000 bytes); the PDF version is 1,310,779 bytes. The transcript page is located at https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/bill-of-rights-transcript. The required bookmark title is exactly 'Bill of Rights Text'. Amendment I full text: 'Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, or prohibiting the free exercise thereof; or abridging the freedom of speech, or of the press; or the right of the people peaceably to assemble, and to petition the Government for a redress of grievances.' Amendment IV full text: 'The right of the people to be secure in their persons, houses, papers, and effects, against unreasonable searches and seizures, shall not be violated, and no Warrants shall issue, but upon probable cause, supported by Oath or affirmation, and particularly describing the place to be searched, and the persons or things to be seized.'
